reactions.story: UGT-catalysed glucosylation of quercetin — converts the hydrophobic yellow plant pigment into a water-soluble glycoside; the basis of bioavailability in onion skin
β-glucosidase — releases bright yellow p-nitrophenol (visible to the naked eye); gold-standard chromogenic glycosidase assay substrate
COMT — SAM-dependent methylation of caffeate 3-OH to give ferulate; controls flux into guaiacyl vs syringyl lignin units in wood formation
ATP-driven glutamate ring closure — makes the pyroglutamyl cap used to stabilise bioactive peptides (TRH, neurotensin) against N-terminal exopeptidases
CYP98A3 — regioselective aromatic C3-hydroxylation of coumaroyl; one of the most highly expressed CYPs in Arabidopsis
Fumarase — anti addition of water to fumarate; gives exclusively (S)-malate; the textbook example of stereospecific hydration in the TCA cycle
PAL — gate enzyme for the phenylpropanoid pathway; makes the cinnamic acid backbone for lignin and all plant flavonoids
Zinc-dependent ADH — oxidises benzyl alcohol to benzaldehyde (bitter almond aroma); archetype of pro-S hydride transfer to NAD⁺
N–S bond hydrolysis — only 1 training example in 46,000. CLIPZyme has essentially zero signal for this bond type.
Xylose isomerase — the same enzyme family industrially converts glucose to fructose for high-fructose corn syrup; metal-assisted hydride shift
Thymidine kinase — phosphorylates the anti-HIV nucleoside analogue stavudine (d4T); first activation step required for its NRTI mechanism of action
P–C bond hydrolysis — only 8 training examples. Phosphonates are synthetic compounds; this unusual bond type is absent from the enzyme screening set.
Ring-cleaving dioxygenase — breaks the aromatic ring of protocatechuate; key enzyme in bacterial degradation of lignin-derived aromatics in contaminated soil
Resveratrol glucosylation — converts the red-wine polyphenol stilbene into its inactive storage glucoside piceid; reversed during grape berry ripening
AADC — decarboxylates tryptophan to tryptamine, the precursor to serotonin, melatonin, and the entire monoterpene-indole alkaloid family
β-xylosidase with halogenated reporter — the bromophenol product can be coupled to Trinder reagent for highly sensitive colorimetric detection
Carnosinase CN2 — cleaves the β-alanyl amide bond; muscle carnosine is the principal intracellular pH buffer during intense exercise
Chalcone isomerase — intramolecular Michael addition with perfect (2S) enantioselectivity and no cofactor; first committed step in flavonoid biosynthesis
CYP2A6 — human hepatic CYP; converts coumarin to the fluorescent umbelliferone; the standard probe reaction for CYP2A6 phenotyping in clinical pharmacogenomics
CAD — final step of monolignol biosynthesis; NADPH-reduction of hydroxycinnamaldehydes to monolignols before oxidative polymerisation into lignin
Tyrosinase — bifunctional copper oxidase that hydroxylates tyrosine to DOPA; responsible for skin pigmentation, mushroom browning, and melanin biosynthesis
CoA-transferase (EC 2.8.3) — transfers thioester without hydrolysis. Fewer than 30 examples in EnzymeMap; no representative in the screening set.
Same enzyme as tryptophan AADC — decarboxylates L-DOPA to dopamine; the key step in the biosynthesis that is bypassed in Parkinson's disease
COMT — SAM-dependent methylation of caffeate 3-OH to give ferulate; controls flux into guaiacyl vs syringyl lignin units in wood formation
Lactase — the enzyme lactose-intolerant people lack; industrial enzyme added to dairy for lactose-free milk production worldwide
Trypsin — serine protease that cleaves C-terminal to Arg/Lys; founding member of the chymotrypsin superfamily; workhorse of proteomics sample preparation
Styrene monooxygenase — epoxidises styrene to enantiopure (S)-styrene oxide; biocatalysis workhorse for chiral pharmaceutical building blocks
IDI — activates the isoprene unit; entry point to the entire terpene and steroid universe comprising >50,000 natural products
PLP-dependent histidine decarboxylase — makes histamine; the antiallergy drug cimetidine inhibits downstream H2 receptors
EC 3.9 aryl-phosphatase — only 3 training examples. Visually identical to acid phosphatase but mechanistically distinct; CLIPZyme cannot distinguish the two.
CYP98A3 — regioselective aromatic C3-hydroxylation of coumaroyl; one of the most highly expressed CYPs in Arabidopsis
Zinc-dependent ADH — oxidises benzyl alcohol to benzaldehyde (bitter almond aroma); archetype of pro-S hydride transfer to NAD⁺
Epoxide hydrolase — Asp-nucleophile-mediated ring opening with retention; stereocomplementary to SMO; combined they give both enantiomers of the chiral diol
Triacylglycerol lipase — serine hydrolase; cleaves ester bond of triglycerides; the most industrially-used enzyme class after protease; active in non-aqueous media
β-glucosidase — releases bright yellow p-nitrophenol (visible to the naked eye); gold-standard chromogenic glycosidase assay substrate
Xylose isomerase — the same enzyme family industrially converts glucose to fructose for high-fructose corn syrup; metal-assisted hydride shift
Glutaminyl cyclase — caps N-terminal glutamine with the pyroglutamyl ring; protects therapeutic peptides (GnRH, neurotensin) against aminopeptidase degradation
ATP-driven glutamate ring closure — makes the pyroglutamyl cap used to stabilise bioactive peptides (TRH, neurotensin) against N-terminal exopeptidases
CAD — final step of monolignol biosynthesis; NADPH-reduction of hydroxycinnamaldehydes to monolignols before oxidative polymerisation into lignin
Fumarase — anti addition of water to fumarate; gives exclusively (S)-malate; the textbook example of stereospecific hydration in the TCA cycle
Chalcone isomerase — intramolecular Michael addition with perfect (2S) enantioselectivity and no cofactor; first committed step in flavonoid biosynthesis
EC 5.99 'other isomerases' — only 2 training examples. Cofactor-free lactol ring opening; no structural homologue exists in the screening set.
Acid phosphatase — p-NPP releases bright yellow p-nitrophenol at 405 nm; gold standard for the widely used colorimetric phosphatase assay in clinical diagnostics
UGT-catalysed glucosylation of quercetin — converts the hydrophobic yellow plant pigment into a water-soluble glycoside; the basis of bioavailability in onion skin
Methylmalonyl-CoA mutase — B₁₂-dependent 1,2-carbon skeleton rearrangement; links branched amino acid catabolism and odd-chain fatty acid oxidation to the TCA cycle
ATP-dependent Trp-His peptide ligase — forms the dipeptide anserine precursor; non-ribosomal-like peptide bond formation between the two most UV-active amino acids
CYP2A6 — human hepatic CYP; converts coumarin to the fluorescent umbelliferone; the standard probe reaction for CYP2A6 phenotyping in clinical pharmacogenomics
Polyamine decarboxylase — spermidine is essential for hypusination of eIF5A, the universal translational regulator across all domains of life
IDI — activates the isoprene unit; entry point to the entire terpene and steroid universe comprising >50,000 natural products
PAL — gate enzyme for the phenylpropanoid pathway; makes the cinnamic acid backbone for lignin and all plant flavonoids
Ferredoxin-NADP reductase acting on a halogenated quinone — only 2 training examples. Bromo-substituted substrate is completely out of distribution for CLIPZyme.
PLP-dependent histidine decarboxylase — makes histamine; the antiallergy drug cimetidine inhibits downstream H2 receptors
Thymidine kinase — phosphorylates the anti-HIV nucleoside analogue stavudine (d4T); first activation step required for its NRTI mechanism of action
Oleate hydratase — Markovnikov-selective hydration of the oleic acid double bond; industrially relevant for making 10-hydroxystearic acid from renewable olive oil
Laccase — multicopper oxidase that converts catechols to quinones; responsible for enzymatic browning of cut fruit and oxidative delignification in white-rot fungi
Carnosinase CN2 — cleaves the β-alanyl amide bond; muscle carnosine is the principal intracellular pH buffer during intense exercise
FHT — 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase; installs 3-OH on naringenin to branch the flavonoid pathway toward kaempferol, quercetin, and anthocyanins
Resveratrol glucosylation — converts the red-wine polyphenol stilbene into its inactive storage glucoside piceid; reversed during grape berry ripening
Ring-cleaving dioxygenase — breaks the aromatic ring of protocatechuate; key enzyme in bacterial degradation of lignin-derived aromatics in contaminated soil
Phenazine oxidase — only 6 training examples. Makes the antibiotic phenazine scaffold in Pseudomonas; entirely out-of-distribution for the screening set.
AADC — decarboxylates tryptophan to tryptamine, the precursor to serotonin, melatonin, and the entire monoterpene-indole alkaloid family
Trypsin — serine protease that cleaves C-terminal to Arg/Lys; founding member of the chymotrypsin superfamily; workhorse of proteomics sample preparation
β-xylosidase with halogenated reporter — the bromophenol product can be coupled to Trinder reagent for highly sensitive colorimetric detection
Acid phosphatase — p-NPP releases bright yellow p-nitrophenol at 405 nm; gold standard for the widely used colorimetric phosphatase assay in clinical diagnostics
Styrene monooxygenase — epoxidises styrene to enantiopure (S)-styrene oxide; biocatalysis workhorse for chiral pharmaceutical building blocks
COMT — SAM-dependent methylation of caffeate 3-OH to give ferulate; controls flux into guaiacyl vs syringyl lignin units in wood formation
TAL — makes p-coumaric acid, the universal building block for flavonoids, stilbenes, coumarins, and all monolignols
CYP98A3 — regioselective aromatic C3-hydroxylation of coumaroyl; one of the most highly expressed CYPs in Arabidopsis
Tyrosinase — bifunctional copper oxidase that hydroxylates tyrosine to DOPA; responsible for skin pigmentation, mushroom browning, and melanin biosynthesis
Zinc-dependent ADH — oxidises benzyl alcohol to benzaldehyde (bitter almond aroma); archetype of pro-S hydride transfer to NAD⁺
Xylose isomerase — the same enzyme family industrially converts glucose to fructose for high-fructose corn syrup; metal-assisted hydride shift
Fatty acid cis-trans isomerase — only 6 training examples. Geometry change only, no cofactor; no structural homologue anywhere in the enzyme screening set.
Lactase — the enzyme lactose-intolerant people lack; industrial enzyme added to dairy for lactose-free milk production worldwide
Glutaminyl cyclase — caps N-terminal glutamine with the pyroglutamyl ring; protects therapeutic peptides (GnRH, neurotensin) against aminopeptidase degradation
Same enzyme as tryptophan AADC — decarboxylates L-DOPA to dopamine; the key step in the biosynthesis that is bypassed in Parkinson's disease
Triacylglycerol lipase — serine hydrolase; cleaves ester bond of triglycerides; the most industrially-used enzyme class after protease; active in non-aqueous media
CYP2A6 — human hepatic CYP; converts coumarin to the fluorescent umbelliferone; the standard probe reaction for CYP2A6 phenotyping in clinical pharmacogenomics
AaRS aminoacylation — only 1 training example of EC 6.1.1.12. The Asp-AMP intermediate chemistry is entirely foreign to the screening enzyme set.
CAD — final step of monolignol biosynthesis; NADPH-reduction of hydroxycinnamaldehydes to monolignols before oxidative polymerisation into lignin
UGT-catalysed glucosylation of quercetin — converts the hydrophobic yellow plant pigment into a water-soluble glycoside; the basis of bioavailability in onion skin
Methylmalonyl-CoA mutase — B₁₂-dependent 1,2-carbon skeleton rearrangement; links branched amino acid catabolism and odd-chain fatty acid oxidation to the TCA cycle
ATP-driven glutamate ring closure — makes the pyroglutamyl cap used to stabilise bioactive peptides (TRH, neurotensin) against N-terminal exopeptidases
Epoxide hydrolase — Asp-nucleophile-mediated ring opening with retention; stereocomplementary to SMO; combined they give both enantiomers of the chiral diol
β-glucosidase — releases bright yellow p-nitrophenol (visible to the naked eye); gold-standard chromogenic glycosidase assay substrate
Chalcone isomerase — intramolecular Michael addition with perfect (2S) enantioselectivity and no cofactor; first committed step in flavonoid biosynthesis
Polyamine decarboxylase — spermidine is essential for hypusination of eIF5A, the universal translational regulator across all domains of life
Carnosinase CN2 — cleaves the β-alanyl amide bond; muscle carnosine is the principal intracellular pH buffer during intense exercise
PLP-dependent histidine decarboxylase — makes histamine; the antiallergy drug cimetidine inhibits downstream H2 receptors
TAL — makes p-coumaric acid, the universal building block for flavonoids, stilbenes, coumarins, and all monolignols
DNA ligase — only 12 training examples. Phosphodiester bond formation in nucleic acid metabolism; no enzyme in the screening set catalyses this reaction type.
Resveratrol glucosylation — converts the red-wine polyphenol stilbene into its inactive storage glucoside piceid; reversed during grape berry ripening
Thymidine kinase — phosphorylates the anti-HIV nucleoside analogue stavudine (d4T); first activation step required for its NRTI mechanism of action
ATP-dependent Trp-His peptide ligase — forms the dipeptide anserine precursor; non-ribosomal-like peptide bond formation between the two most UV-active amino acids
FHT — 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase; installs 3-OH on naringenin to branch the flavonoid pathway toward kaempferol, quercetin, and anthocyanins
IDI — activates the isoprene unit; entry point to the entire terpene and steroid universe comprising >50,000 natural products
